The Proposal -
To promote education among tribals in the hilly and remote areas of the state of Maharashtra.
The government started the "Ashramshala Group" scheme from the year 1972-73.
There are a total of 556 aided ashramshalas run by NGOs out of which 37 are
Primary Aided Ashramshalas (Classes 1 to 7) and 319 Secondary Aided Ashramshalas
(Classes 1 to 10) are in progress. As well as 200 junior colleges attached to secondary ashramshalas.
(Classes 1 to 12) are in progress. 5 subsidized elementary from this academic year 2026-27
Upgradation of Ashramshalas from 8th to 10th standard and 19 Aided Secondary Ashramshalas.
Affiliated Junior Colleges (Classes 11 and 12, Arts and Science stream)
It was under the consideration of the Government.
Cabinet approves grant-in-aid to 5 NGOs operating in the State under Tribal Development Department
Department of Upgradation of Primary Ashramshalas and 19 Aided Secondary Ashramshalas
The proposal has been approved. Accordingly, the Government is now taking the following decisions in this regard.
The Government's Decision-
Pursuant to the approval given by the Hon'ble Cabinet in its meeting dated 05.05.2026

5. To raise the standard of education in the aided ashrams where grading has been approved.
It's necessary. In view of this, the annual result of the students of such ashramshalas is at least 80.

Ashramshala, Billadi, T.G. Dhule, as may be prescribed by the Government from time to time.

Comply with the terms, conditions, and regulations set forth.

10. If it is found that the conditions, conditions and rules prescribed by the Government are not complied with.
Also, if there are complaints of malpractice / mismanagement regarding the aforesaid Ashramshala

will be binding on the organization.
